title: Makefile: fail early with a clear message on Intel Mac
body:
Building on darwin/amd64 currently fails deep in the Go toolchain with
"all Go files in directory excluded by build constraints" because
Podman 6 dropped Intel Mac support. The error gives no indication of
what actually went wrong, which is a bad experience for someone who
just cloned the repo and ran make.

This adds a $(error) guard in the Makefile that fires when
GOOS/GOARCH is darwin/amd64, producing a one-liner that says Intel Mac
is no longer supported and suggests alternatives.

Also added a note in CONTRIBUTING.md under the contributing section.

Tested by overriding GOOS/GOARCH in a standalone Makefile snippet —
darwin/amd64 triggers the error, darwin/arm64 and linux/amd64 pass
through.

Fixes #28416

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

Should not it still be possible to cross compile at least `podman-remote` for darwin/arm64 or `windows/amd64` or `windows/arm64` while still using Intel Macs? I have Intel Mac, but had no time to verify these assumptions yet.

If this is not tested as part of CI doesn't mean the platform is incapable of this and not having target support for host should not be an immediate error.

Being honest Intel Macs are on their way out, so, this comment is of a less importance, but same can be said about having dedicated error messages for the platform being phased out.

> Should not it still be possible to cross compile at least `podman-remote` for darwin/arm64 or `windows/amd64` or `windows/arm64` while still using Intel Macs? I have Intel Mac, but had no time to verify these assumptions yet.
> 

Yeah I guess instead of failing all make operations the check should be for GOOS/GOARCH in `$(SRCBINDIR)/podman$(BINSFX):` to block only the podman-remote build there

Good points, both of you. Moved the check from a global $(error) into the $(SRCBINDIR)/podman$(BINSFX) target and switched to GOOS/GOARCH so it only fires when actually building a darwin/amd64 binary. An Intel Mac user can still cross-compile for linux or windows with GOOS=linux etc.
Updated the CONTRIBUTING.md note — it now says cross-compiling for other targets still works on Intel Mac, only native darwin/amd64 binaries are blocked.

You're right - updated again. The note now says darwin/amd64 binaries can't be built on any host, not just Intel Macs. Also confirmed that `GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 make podman-remote` from an Intel Mac would still work (the check only blocks darwin/amd64).
